# TAO-034: Pseudo-Workspace Slot Strategy

- ID: `TAO-034`
- Status: `backlog`
- Priority: `P2`
- Depends on: `TAO-033`

## Problem

Teams need a way to run multiple concurrent ticket sessions without coupling to git worktree branch constraints.

## Scope

- Define a pseudo-workspace slot model that maps issue/session work to dedicated absolute local paths.
- Specify lifecycle operations for slot allocate/reuse/release.
- Define sync and branch strategy constraints without requiring git worktree.

## Out of Scope

- Full VCS automation implementation.
- Remote execution scheduler.

## Acceptance Criteria

- Slot model and lifecycle are specified with clear invariants.
- CWD resolution policy can consume slot-assigned paths as project/issue/runtime inputs.

## Verification

- Spec review walkthrough with concrete scenario matrix.
019c54b8-b16d-78a1-95f7-e82293d041d5.author: migration

019c54b8-b046-7ef2-aa59-4e27c0151485.body_markdown:
# TAO-006: Startup Reconciliation and Orphan Handling

- ID: `TAO-006`
- Status: `done`
- Priority: `P1`
- Depends on: `TAO-005`

## Problem

Crashes or forced exits can leave stale running sessions and orphaned processes.

## Scope

- Reconcile persisted `running` sessions against live PIDs/PTYs on startup.
- Reattach when possible.
- Mark unrecoverable sessions as `terminated_orphaned` with reason code.

## Acceptance Criteria

- No stale `running` sessions remain after reconciliation.
- Recovery outcome is recorded per session.

## Verification

- Fault-injection tests for crash/restart scenarios.

Verification completed:

- `cargo fmt --all --check` passed.
- `cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings` passed.
- `cargo test --workspace` passed.
019c54b8-b046-7ef2-aa59-4e27c0151485.author: migration

019c54b8-b192-7a50-821c-a4bb94263c7f.body_markdown:
# TAO-038: Unified `toa` Command Tree and Ticket CLI

- ID: `TAO-038`
- Status: `in_progress`
- Priority: `P1`
- Depends on: `TAO-015`, `TAO-016`, `TAO-037`

## Problem

Programmatic ticket/project workflows are fragmented between TUI interactions and partial transport handlers, making automation and scripted operations inconsistent.

## Scope

- Introduce a unified `toa` command tree as the primary operator and automation interface.
- Add issue/project CLI subcommands backed by canonical in-process handlers.
- Preserve existing TUI launch behavior under the new command surface.
- Provide machine-friendly JSON output mode for script and agent usage.

## Out of Scope

- Full MCP tool server implementation.
- HTTP transport parity work.
- Internal crate/package renaming across the entire workspace.

## Acceptance Criteria

- `toa tui` continues to run the current TUI workflow.
- `toa issue` and `toa project` expose basic create/list/get/update flows.
- CLI commands operate on persisted state and return actionable errors.
- JSON output mode is available for programmatic consumers.

## Verification

- Unit tests for CLI argument parsing and command routing.
- Command-level tests for issue/project operations.
- `cargo fmt --all --check`
- `cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings`
- `cargo test --workspace`

## Follow-up Note

- Full internal naming migration (crate/package-level rename to Team of Agents) is deferred until UX/API surfaces stabilize.

## Progress Update (2026-02-11)

- Landed:
  - `toa` binary naming as primary command entrypoint,
  - unified command tree bootstrap with `tui`, `issue`, `project`, and `mcp` command groups,
  - expanded issue/project CLI operations over persisted state:
    - issues: `create`, `list`, `get`, `move`, `assign-project`, `set-cwd`, `clear-cwd`, `delete`,
    - projects: `create`, `list`, `get`, `set-key`, `set-repo-path`, `clear-repo-path`,
  - JSON output mode for issue/project command groups.
  - internal operation helpers introduced to reduce CLI/MCP behavior drift across issue/project workflows.
- Remaining:
  - improve command-level coverage and richer filtering/update operations,
  - continue CLI UX hardening (output consistency, richer filters, and batch-friendly ergonomics),
  - finalize comments ergonomics and pagination defaults in CLI (`TAO-040`).
019c54b8-b192-7a50-821c-a4bb94263c7f.author: migration

# TAO-036: TUI CWD Configuration Flows

- ID: `TAO-036`
- Status: `done`
- Priority: `P1`
- Depends on: `TAO-033`

## Problem

After adding strict launch CWD policy, operators need first-class TUI actions to set project and issue path settings without editing snapshots manually.

## Scope

- Add TUI key flows to set/clear selected issue project repo path.
- Add TUI key flows to set/clear selected issue cwd override.
- Auto-attach newly created issues to a default project to keep launch flows usable.
- Validate configured paths are absolute existing directories.
- Add mouse action bar for core issue/session operations.
- Support shortcut key overrides via `TAO_KEY_*` environment variables.

## Out of Scope

- Full project management UI.
- Clone/bootstrap automation and pseudo-workspace allocator.

## Acceptance Criteria

- Operators can update project repo path from TUI.
- Operators can update issue cwd override from TUI.
- New issues are attached to a default project when no explicit project is selected.
- Invalid relative paths are rejected with actionable errors.

## Verification

- Unit tests for path update flows and validation.
- `cargo fmt --all --check`
- `cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings`
- `cargo test --workspace`

# TAO-033: Session CWD Resolution Policy and Launch Guard

- ID: `TAO-033`
- Status: `done`
- Priority: `P1`
- Depends on: `TAO-010`, `TAO-012`, `TAO-013`, `TAO-015`

## Problem

Agent launch CWD was effectively a single global value and could silently run from an unintended directory.

## Scope

- Define and implement absolute-path-only CWD resolution tiers:
  - runtime override,
  - issue override,
  - project repository path.
- Block launch when no valid path can be resolved.
- Persist project/issue path metadata in API snapshot model for policy enforcement.
- Add tests that cover precedence and rejection of relative paths.

## Out of Scope

- Git worktree-native mechanics.
- Clone/orchestration workflows for repo bootstrap.
- Multi-slot pseudo-workspace allocator.

## Acceptance Criteria

- Launch path resolution order is deterministic: `runtime > issue > project`.
- Only absolute existing directories are accepted for launches.
- Missing/invalid path blocks launch with actionable status errors.

## Verification

- Unit tests for launch path precedence and validation edge cases.
- `cargo fmt --all --check`
- `cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings`
- `cargo test --workspace`

Verification completed:

- `cargo fmt --all --check` passed.
- `cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings` passed.
- `cargo test --workspace` passed.
019c54b8-b161-7982-abfc-126f1d5763bb.author: migration

# TAO-039: MCP Server Ticket and Project Tools

- ID: `TAO-039`
- Status: `in_progress`
- Priority: `P1`
- Depends on: `TAO-038`

## Problem

Agent-driven workflows need a tool protocol endpoint for issue/project operations without screen-scraping TUI output.

## Scope

- Add `toa mcp serve` entrypoint.
- Expose ticket/project operations as MCP tools.
- Reuse existing core handlers to keep behavior parity with CLI.

## Acceptance Criteria

- MCP server starts via `toa mcp serve`.
- Agents can list/create/update issues and projects through tools.
- Tool behavior is consistent with `toa issue` and `toa project` commands.

## Verification

- Tool contract tests for success and error paths.
- `cargo fmt --all --check`
- `cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings`
- `cargo test --workspace`

## Progress Update (2026-02-11)

- Landed:
  - `toa mcp serve` now starts a stdio MCP-style JSON-RPC loop,
  - implemented `tools/list` and `tools/call` handlers,
  - exposed issue/project tool set with CLI parity:
    - issues: `issue_list`, `issue_get`, `issue_create`, `issue_move`, `issue_assign_project`, `issue_set_cwd`, `issue_clear_cwd`, `issue_delete`,
    - projects: `project_list`, `project_get`, `project_create`, `project_set_key`, `project_set_repo_path`, `project_clear_repo_path`,
  - persisted state integration for mutating tool calls.
  - resource discovery and read support via MCP:
    - `resources/list`,
    - `resources/read` for `toa://projects`, `toa://issues`, `toa://health`.
  - operator reference doc added at `docs/mcp-tools.md`.
  - MCP tool handlers now reuse shared issue/project operation helpers aligned with CLI flows.
  - curated MCP tool surface to avoid tool-count explosion:
    - kept core mutation/navigation tools,
    - moved lower-value operations to CLI-first guidance,
    - deprecated tools now return explicit MCP errors with CLI direction.
  - comments tools/resources added while preserving curated MCP scope:
    - tools: `comment_add`, `comment_list`,
    - resources: `toa://issues/{issue}/comments`, `toa://projects/{project}/comments`.
- Remaining:
  - tighten protocol coverage and compatibility details,
  - add broader negative-path and contract tests for tool schemas/validation,
  - keep MCP surface curated while comments capabilities are added via `TAO-040`.
019c54b8-b19e-7363-a31e-d0439f9bfbf4.author: migration

- Improve issue/project creation affordances in TUI.
- Improve issue-first navigation speed and discoverability.
- Surface continuity metadata (sessions/comments) without overwhelming the pane.

## Out of Scope

- Full markdown rendering.
- Cross-platform GUI redesign.

## Acceptance Criteria

- Creating issues/projects feels explicit and low-friction.
- Navigating and selecting issues is faster and clearer.
- Session continuity and comment context are visible at-a-glance.

## Verification

- Unit tests for new navigation/input interactions.
- `cargo fmt --all --check`
- `cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings`
- `cargo test --workspace`

## Progress Update (2026-02-12)

- Landed:
  - top header now surfaces workspace-level issue/project counts and active subview,
  - active subview action button is visually highlighted,
  - issue rows now include project key + comment count metadata,
  - project rows now include comment count metadata,
  - added a dedicated context line above status with selected issue/project continuity details,
  - normal-mode hints are shorter and subview-specific.
  - quick comment affordances added in normal mode:
    - `a` enters markdown comment input for selected issue/project,
    - `l` shows recent comment preview for selected issue/project,
    - action bar includes `+ Comment` for mouse-first flow.
  - right pane can now toggle between session output and comments feed (`t`),
  - creation prompt ergonomics improved:
    - issues accept `<title> | <project optional>`,
    - projects accept `<KEY> | <NAME>`.
  - input modes now surface mini-form previews in the right pane (issue/project/comment) with required-field hints and save/cancel cues.
  - action bar now adapts to active subview to reduce noise:
    - issues view emphasizes launch/session actions,
    - projects view emphasizes project management actions.
  - floating input modal now appears for issue/project/comment entry, rendered with ratatui `Block`/`Paragraph` widgets,
  - separators and modal borders use solid lines for clearer visual structure.
  - issue creation now follows a step flow in modal form:
    - title entry,
    - optional description entry,
    - project picker with fuzzy filter + keyboard selection (`j/k`).
- Remaining:
  - creation UX still needs dedicated modal polish for issue/project forms,
  - continue reducing cognitive load in action bar/hints for first-time operators.

# TAO-025: Embedded Terminal Surface v1

- ID: `TAO-025`
- Status: `done`
- Priority: `P0`
- Depends on: `TAO-004`, `TAO-009`, `TAO-012`, `TAO-013`

## Problem

Interactive coding agents (OpenCode, Claude Code) emit full terminal control streams and require pane rendering fidelity beyond line-oriented logs.

## Scope

- Introduce a dedicated terminal-surface module for session panes.
- Parse PTY output using a VT parser/state machine and maintain per-session screen state.
- Render the session pane from screen state (cursor addressing, alternate screen, SGR attributes, scrollback viewport).
- Route pane input and resize events with terminal semantics expected by interactive tools.
- Ensure shutdown/restart handling prevents unmanaged orphaned agent processes in normal exit paths.

## Out of Scope

- Replacing tmux/zellij as a standalone multiplexer.
- Persisting orchestrator sessions as zellij sessions.
- Remote transport redesign.

## Acceptance Criteria

- Launching OpenCode from an issue renders visible interactive UI in the embedded pane.
- Re-attaching to an existing live primary session does not freeze the TUI.
- Session pane resize updates propagate and the agent UI reflows correctly.
- Exiting the TUI performs session lifecycle cleanup according to runtime policy.

## Verification

- Integration test or scripted smoke test that launches OpenCode and validates in-pane render updates.
- Manual nested-terminal test (`tmux`/`zellij`) confirms no host terminal corruption.

- `cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings`
- `cargo test --workspace`
